turns.student: What brings you in today?
turns.patient: I've been experiencing a persistent cough and difficulty breathing for about 2 weeks now. It's been really affecting my daily activities, especially since I'm an elementary school teacher and work with young children every day.

turns.student: Can you describe the main symptom you're experiencing?
turns.patient: The cough started off as a dry, annoying cough that just wouldn't go away. But over the past 5 days or so, it's become productive - I'm coughing up yellowish-green sputum, which is pretty gross and concerning to me. The difficulty breathing part is also getting worse; I feel like I'm short of breath even when I'm not exerting myself, and it's really taking a toll on my energy levels.

turns.student: When did this start?
turns.patient: It started about 2 weeks ago, but to be honest, it was more of a nuisance at first. The dry cough was annoying me, but I just thought it would go away with time. It wasn't until it turned productive and I started coughing up all this stuff that I realized something was really off.

turns.student: How often does it happen?
turns.patient: It's constant, to be honest. I'm coughing on a regular basis throughout the day, even when I'm just sitting still or walking around my apartment. It's like my body is just constantly trying to clear out something, but I don't know what. And then there are these periods where it gets really bad and I feel like I'm wheezing or struggling to catch my breath. That's when it really scares me.

turns.student: How severe is it on a scale of 1 to 10?
turns.patient: I'd say it's an 8 out of 10, maybe even higher some days. The constant coughing and wheezing are exhausting, both physically and mentally. It's also hard for me to sleep at night because I'm coughing so much, and that just makes me feel more tired and run down the next day.

turns.student: Do you notice any other symptoms when it happens?
turns.patient: Yes, I've been experiencing a low-grade fever, usually around 38.2°C (100.8°F), which is pretty intermittent but seems to be worse in the evenings. And I've also had some fatigue and malaise, which has caused me to miss three days of work already.

turns.student: Does it get worse with any particular activity?
turns.patient: To be honest, the coughing and difficulty breathing seem to worsen when I'm around young children at school or in crowded spaces. I think being around so many germs might trigger my symptoms, but it's hard to say for sure. The fatigue also gets worse if I try to exert myself too much, like carrying groceries up the stairs or doing household chores.

turns.student: Do you have any sweating, nausea, or dizziness with it?
turns.patient: No, I don't experience any sweating, nausea, or dizziness. Just the persistent cough and difficulty breathing, which are really affecting my daily life right now.

turns.student: Does the pain or discomfort spread to any other part of your body?
turns.patient: No, it's just localized to my chest and back area where I feel like I'm getting this tightness and pressure. The coughing itself is what seems to be causing most of the discomfort.

turns.student: Do you have any swelling in your legs or feet?
turns.patient: No, I don't have any swelling in my legs or feet. But I do want to mention that I've been taking over-the-counter dextromethorphan for the past week to try and manage the cough, but it hasn't really helped much. And I'm also still on cetirizine, which is usually fine for my seasonal allergies, but I'm not sure if it's helping with this specific issue.

turns.student: What medications are you currently taking?
turns.patient: I've been taking over-the-counter dextromethorphan for the past week to try and manage the cough, and I'm also still on cetirizine for my seasonal allergies. The dextromethorphan hasn't really helped much, but I'm not sure if it's supposed to work this quickly or not.

turns.student: Do you have any medical conditions like high blood pressure or diabetes?
turns.patient: No, I don't have any pre-existing medical conditions like high blood pressure or diabetes. But I do want to mention that my mother has asthma, which might be relevant since I've been experiencing some wheezing and difficulty breathing myself.

turns.student: Have you ever had your cholesterol checked?
turns.patient: No, I haven't had my cholesterol checked recently. To be honest, with everything going on with this cough and breathing issue, it hasn't even crossed my mind to check my cholesterol levels.

turns.student: Do you smoke or drink alcohol?
turns.patient:
I don't smoke at all. As for drinking, I do have a glass of wine on occasion, but only 1-2 glasses on weekends when I'm out with friends or at dinner parties. It's not something I do regularly, and definitely not during the week.

Also, just to add, my partner has had a mild cold recently, so we've been trying to take precautions to avoid exposing each other to germs.

turns.student: How would you describe your diet and exercise habits?
turns.patient:
I'd say my diet is pretty normal - I try to eat healthy most of the time, but with this cough and breathing issue going on, I've been eating a lot more comfort foods like pasta and soup because they're easy to digest. As for exercise, I used to be pretty active, but lately, I've been feeling too tired to do much of anything physical. Even simple tasks like carrying groceries or doing chores have become exhausting for me.

Also, just to add, my mother has asthma, which might be relevant since I've been experiencing some wheezing and difficulty breathing myself.

turns.student: Has anyone in your family had heart disease or heart attacks?
turns.patient: I don't think that's directly related to what's going on with me. My mom has asthma, like I mentioned earlier, but I'm not aware of any history of heart disease or heart attacks in my family.

turns.student: Has anyone in your family had other serious medical conditions?
turns.patient: My maternal grandmother actually passed away from lung cancer, which might be worth mentioning since I've been experiencing some respiratory issues. But aside from that, I'm not aware of any significant family history of serious medical conditions.

turns.student: When was the last time you saw a doctor?
turns.patient: I think my last physical exam was about a year ago, everything seemed normal back then. My doctor didn't mention anything out of the ordinary during that visit, so I'm not sure if this cough and breathing issue might be related to something from then or if it's just a new development.

turns.student: Do you have any allergies to medications?
turns.patient: I do have an allergy to penicillin - it causes a rash on my skin whenever I take it. Other than that, I'm not aware of any other allergies to medications.

turns.student: Is there anything else you think I should know about your health?
turns.patient:
I'd like to add that several students in my class have been sick with upper respiratory infections in the past month, so it's possible that whatever is going on might be contagious. And because I work with young children every day, I'm worried that I might be exposing them to something that could make them sick too.

Also, I should mention that I've had seasonal allergies since I was a teenager, and I usually take cetirizine as needed during allergy season. But this time around, the coughing and difficulty breathing seem unrelated to my allergies, even though I'm still on the same medication.
